title: v1.3.0 released: graph-ranked retrieval, the NOW view, and the quieter agent loop are on npm
context: Shipped from the roadmap per rb_cut_release_01: preflight green (suite + typecheck + drift), version bump + lockfile, outcome-language changelog + README banner, chore(release) commit, explicit tag push (the --follow-tags gotcha), release.yml green, npm view confirms 1.3.0 with provenance.
decision: v1.3.0 is live on npm. Rollout status: the OFFICE machine's global update hit the known-class blocker — EBUSY on tree-sitter.node because this very session's MCP server holds the native binding (recorded as bug_ebusy_treesitter, first bug in the public store; lineage ties it to the v1.0.0 better-sqlite3 EPERM class; candidate fix: lazy-load tree-sitter so idle servers hold no native handles). Local hooks go live after this session closes + one `npm i -g @davesheffer/hunch`; the home machine installs clean. Until then the hooks run 1.2.3 — expect one more stale-hook duplicate draft per commit, which `hunch review --reject-duplicates` (source) sweeps.
consequences: First release where the changelog was written against decisions recorded the same week — every claim in it resolves to a dec_ id in the graph
bug_ebusy_treesitter seeds bug lineage in the public store; the lazy-load fix is a natural next roadmap item if the friction repeats
